impact: T-032 decided not to refactor inline styles for CSP reasons (kept style-src unsafe-inline). But the codebase has clear design-system hygiene wins independent of CSP. The top-5 static, non-interpolated inline-style patterns alone cover ~250 of the 3,237 occurrences and are mechanically sed-replaceable: (a) `font-size:10px;color:var(--muted);` — 68 sites, (b) `color:var(--muted);` — 58 sites, (c) `color:var(--text);` — 46 sites, (d) `padding:0.75rem;` — 40 sites, (e) `font-size:9px;letter-spacing:0.12em;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--muted);` — 39 sites (clearly a 'section eyebrow label' utility). Adding 5 utility classes to base.html's <style> block (.muted, .text-label, .section-eyebrow, etc.) and a sed pass to swap them would shrink the inline-style count by ~8% with zero functional risk. Pure DX/maintainability win, not security. Independent of T-033.
resolution:
Defined 5 utility classes in `app/templates/base.html` and swept all standalone occurrences of the top-5 inline-style patterns across the repo.

**Utility classes added** (inside the existing `<style nonce>` block):
- `.b-muted` — `color: var(--muted)`
- `.b-text` — `color: var(--text)`
- `.b-pad-md` — `padding: 0.75rem`
- `.b-muted-sm` — `font-size: 10px; color: var(--muted)`
- `.b-eyebrow` — `font-size: 9px; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--muted)` (section eyebrow label)

**Sweep:** Python script at `/tmp/iss028_migrate.py` walked `app/templates/**/*.html`, found every `<tag … style="EXACT">` where `EXACT` matched one of the five patterns above, and either:
- Appended the new class to an existing `class=` attribute and dropped the `style=` entirely, or
- Created a `class=` attribute when none existed.

**Result:** 251 sites in 67 templates swept. All standalone occurrences for these five patterns now return 0 hits in a fresh grep. All other inline-style declarations (the ~3,000 embedded occurrences where the pattern is mixed with other CSS in a larger style string) are left untouched — those need careful per-site review and are out of scope for this DX-only cleanup.

**Verification:** Full Jinja parse pass over all 100 user-facing templates is clean (the 3 errors flagged are pre-existing — `partials/assets_results.html`, `partials/corp_detail.html`, `status_data.html` use app-registered Jinja custom filters not exposed to an isolated Environment).

**Future contributors:** prefer the utility classes for new code matching any of the five patterns. The longer compound patterns (eyebrow, muted-sm) are checked first by the sweep script, so adding new sites in either form is safe.

Commit: pending deploy.
title: Design-system hygiene: consolidate top-5 repeated inline-style patterns into utility classes
